response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has no overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU/AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI-adjacent work at re:cinq is software-layer agent infrastructure (MCP frameworks), not hardware-level AI accelerator performance engineering. With a BS in Computer Science and 17+ years of experience, he meets the tenure threshold, but the domain mismatch is fundamental and not bridgeable without a significant career pivot.
strengths: 17+ years of continuous engineering experience, meeting the BS+8yr qualification bar
Low-latency and high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby using async I/O, fibers, and performance-oriented DLang
Some systems-level C/C++ work at National Instruments on measurement software tooling
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely absent
No AI/ML or deep learning workload expertise; AI work at re:cinq is software agent infrastructure, not accelerator optimization
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design background at any point in career

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN expertise
